content:
<!-- Shared notice board. Keep to <=2-3 short bullets; delete bullets no longer true. -->

- **SNAP `tensorObjAssoc`/`hK_lhs` — ~10 iters stuck, structurally proven blocked; the loop executes a structural refactor next iter.** Every tactic reduces to the same dual-`MonoidalCategory`-instance μ whnf-bomb (now proven: even an isolated single-whisker `change` bombs alone). The autonomous fix: rewire the hand-built `tensorObj*` defs onto the `LocalizedMonoidal` synonym ⊗ so the boundary vanishes. (Deleting the duplicate instance is NOT a fix — load-bearing, 51-site cascade.) No user action; steer via `USER_HINTS.md`.
- **FBC iterated-mate glue — live frontier, harder than a one-shot.** Both b2 mate legs are closed and a new algebraic component bridge landed this iter, but the geometric `.app (tilde M)` bridge kernel-bombs (sheafification whnf), so the glue gets a dedicated multi-step nat-transformation lane (+ effort-breaker). Converging, not blocked. No action needed.
- **FBC `base_change_mate_*` riders — flagged for a dedicated excision iter (recurring).** This "dead" apparatus is still wired into the live seeds, carries a misplaced heartbeat option, and cites lemmas that were never written; the loop will excise + re-route in a dedicated planner-level iter (not a prover task). No user action.
